_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description:
You can:
- work with all business areas to define the problems that need to be solved
- ensure roadmaps meet organisational strategic aims and that responsibility for delivery of the roadmap is shared appropriately across business areas
- decide when to manage, maintain or evolve products and services to deliver organisational outcomes and support seamless user journeys
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- partnering with senior leaders to determine how potential blockers in roadmaps will be managed 
- managing expectations on what the organisation has the capacity to deliver and pushing back when needed
- directing and agreeing resource, guidance and investment to ensure opportunities for innovation are acted upon, and live products and services are continuously developed
reference_document_element_description: Skill in negotiating strategic vendor partnerships for data and AI platforms, including commercial terms, roadmap alignment, dependency management, and exit strategy planning.
comments: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI LS domain. LS-S-013 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-T-004 (AB, cosine: 0.564), DQ-T-010 (DQ, cosine: 0.541).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.3/10 (raw 0.431). Shared vocabulary: [roadmap, skill, strategic]. DDaT-only terms [across, acted, agreeing, aims, appropriately, areas] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, alignment, commercial, data, dependency, exit] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.835 (P=0.810, R=0.863). R exceeds P by 0.053 — the WIDAI KSA's 22 words are well-captured by the DDaT element's 120 words, but the DDaT element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance:
NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "You can:
- work with all business areas to define the problems that need to be s" and "Skill in negotiating strategic vendor partnerships for data and AI platforms, in" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.537 > STS 0.431 (gap +0.106). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [roadmap, skill, strategic] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.1%. Shared tokens: [roadmap, skill, strategic]. DDaT-only: [across, acted, agreeing, aims, appropriately, areas]. WIDAI-only: [ai, alignment, commercial, data, dependency, exit].

signals.detail: STS 0.431 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.
